title: When Reflecting on Past Events: - List the Facts: Write Down Both Positive and
subtitle: Spot Euphoric Recall
description:
When reflecting on past events:
- List the facts: Write down both positive and negative aspects of the memory.
- Get perspective: Talk to others who shared the experience to balance your view.
- Be realistic: Remind yourself that every memory has highs and lows.

Example: If you recall an old job fondly, note the challenges you faced there to keep things balanced.

howTo.title: Define the metric baseline
howTo.summary: Identify and record the key metrics for your memories, focusing on the number of facts and time spent reflecting. Aim for 6 to 10 facts and 10 to 60 minutes per memory.

howTo.title: Create a fact list
howTo.summary: Select a memory and spend 10 minutes listing 5 to 8 concrete facts. Include both positive and negative aspects to provide a balanced view.

howTo.title: Gather external perspectives
howTo.summary: Reach out to one or two co-witnesses who shared the experience. Ask specific questions to corroborate facts and gain different perceptions.

howTo.title: Analyze and reflect
howTo.summary: Review the facts and perspectives gathered. Assign a confidence score to the memory and consider how it influences your current decisions.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results in the Brali LifeOS app to track your progress and insights. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/euphoric-recall-reality-check/.
